title: Date-range match filtering exists in the query layer but is not exposed as an MCP tool parameter
evidence: queries.py:36-39 supports date_from/date_to, but the server.py:26 find_matches tool only exposes season, so an LLM client cannot filter by an arbitrary date range
suggestion: Add date_from/date_to params to the find_matches MCP tool to fully surface the spec's 'by date range' capability

title: Club-based player search works but is thin for Brazilian clubs due to FIFA dataset coverage
evidence: queries.py:179-181 filters players by canonical club_key and works, but README.md notes the fifa_data.csv licenses few Brazilian clubs, so 'players at Brazilian clubs' queries return sparse results
suggestion: Documented limitation is acceptable; optionally supplement with an additional club-roster source

title: Standings unconditionally labels row 1 'Champion' even for incomplete/partial seasons
evidence: server.py:64 hardcodes ' - Champion' for i==1 regardless of whether the season's matches are complete
suggestion: Only apply the champion label when the season is known-complete, or drop it

title: Strong TDD structure satisfying the prompt factor: 49 unit tests across 4 files paralleling each module
evidence: tests/test_queries.py uses table-driven fixtures, tests/test_server.py exercises the async MCP tools; _agent_stdout.log reports red/green/refactor cycle and two bugs (double-counted standings, int(NaN) crash) caught during it
